name: Heinrich v. [New York Court of Appeals case by Judge Cardozo]
summary: A New York Court of Appeals decision by Judge Cardozo that described checks lost behind a radiator at a post office as having miscarried, illustrating the broad historical usage of the term.
significance: Cited by petitioner to show that pre-FTCA judicial authorities used 'loss' and 'miscarriage' interchangeably, including describing checks stuck behind a radiator at a post office as having 'miscarried.'
